amendments.normalized_body: The `StartJournalDetails` struct in `src/adapters/fs.rs` (line 2280) duplicates the field set of `StartJournalDetailsPayload` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs` (line 354). If a new field is added to the model's rendering function without updating the fs adapter's parsing struct, `StartJournalDetails::merge` will silently discard the unrecognized field during repair. Consider either: (a) making the model's `StartJournalDetailsPayload` public and adding `Deserialize` so fs.rs can reuse it, or (b) adding a compile-time or test assertion that the two structs stay in sync. The same issue already exists for `CompletionJournalDetails` — both pairs should be addressed together.

In `FsMilestoneJournalStore::repairable_start_event` (fs.rs line 2482), if `existing.details` is `None`, the `?` operator causes the entire function to return `None`, which means the repair is silently skipped and a new (duplicate) event is appended. For `BeadStarted` events produced by this code, `details` is always `Some`, so this cannot happen today. However, adding an explicit guard or debug assertion (`debug_assert!(existing.details.is_some())`) would make the invariant self-documenting and catch regressions where a start event is somehow written without details.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2984-3024` rejects `update_task_run` whenever the caller supplies a `run_id` but the only open match is the same bead/project attempt stored without a `run_id`. That means a start recorded before the controller knew the Ralph run ID cannot be finalized later with the newly-known `run_id` or `plan_hash`, even though `started_at` is also available and the selected row could be backfilled once matched (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3186-3209`). In that recovery path the completion fails instead of updating the existing attempt.

`persist_plan` stores the canonical plan hash in the milestone snapshot (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:365-371`), but both `record_bead_start` and `update_task_run` ignore that state and just forward an optional caller-supplied `plan_hash` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:401-446`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:556-568`). The FS adapter then writes `TaskRunEntry { plan_hash: None }` whenever the caller omits it (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2928-2937`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:3189-3209`). After a plan has been persisted, the system already knows which plan version is executing, so lineage can still lose the required plan linkage.

The change still carries a large legacy-repair layer even though the task explicitly says there is no old milestone NDJSON to migrate. Examples: named/runless fallback matching in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:444-519`, attempt collapsing for legacy start/completion pairs in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:521-611`, journal row rewriting in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2445-2543`, and in-place backfill helpers for missing run metadata in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2661-2733`. That reintroduces the exact backfill/repair behavior the acceptance criteria said to remove, and it makes the audit trail harder to reason about because incorrect rows are silently merged or rewritten instead of failing loudly.

payload.summary: Final-review restart cap reached (20/20); force-completing instead of restarting with 3 accepted amendment(s).
